mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when FEDFUNDS rises.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 4.0 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0%, or the signal loses predictive power during expansionary phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and credit availability, which drives corporate revenue growth and yields positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a positive Rank IC greater than 0.04 over rolling 5-year windows, or housing starts decline while retail sales rise without affecting returns.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a late-cycle expansion reflects capacity utilization nearing peaks, which supports corporate profit margins and generates positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during late-cycle phases but S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain negative in over 60% of cases, or the mechanism reverses direction during early-cycle recoveries.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above a 0.4% monthly threshold forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are positive in more than 50% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 0.4% threshold, or the signal shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market that boosts consumer spending power and drives positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields a negative or zero Rank IC over rolling 3-year periods, or payrolls rise while unemployment falls without generating positive forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth directly increases corporate earnings expectations, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no correlation with 20-day forward returns, or the Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase indicates persistent supply chain bottlenecks that compress profit margins and trigger negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during post-shock recoveries but S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in over 65% of instances, or the signal loses predictive power outside this specific regime.
